first_run.rationale: The scores are identical (5/10), and the reasoning across the justification, strengths, and concerns sections is substantively the same. Both evaluations correctly identify the candidate as a highly experienced engineer but note a critical lack of specific experience in experimentation systems, which is the core reason for the low score. The minor differences in wording are well within the bounds of normal run-to-run variation and do not reflect any change in the assessment of the candidate's skills or experience based on the change of all job locations to Brazil.

median_run.rationale: The two evaluations are nearly identical in substance. Both award the same score and highlight the same core strengths (extensive senior-level experience) and the same core weakness (lack of specific experience in experimentation platforms, which is central to the role). The minor differences in wording and the specific examples of missing experience (e.g., Python vs. feature flagging) are well within the bounds of normal run-to-run variation and represent different ways of expressing the same fundamental gap in the candidate's profile. There is no evidence that the change in location from Europe/North America to Brazil influenced the assessment.
